g4_revenue_trend.description: Revenue flat (-0.1%), organic +0.9% but VOLUME -0.3% — price masking volume decline. Meals & Desserts segment -6.5%.

g5_gross_margin_trend.description: Reported 26.6% (2014) vs 37.4% (2013) — pension accounting distortion, normalized ~34-35%, but 3-year trend declining from 38%→36%→35%.

g7_moat.description: Brands are 'recognized' not 'loved.' Oscar Mayer (processed meat, health trend headwind), Maxwell House (losing to premium/K-Cup), Jell-O (category dying), Velveeta (processed cheese), Planters (commodity nuts). Only Philadelphia cream cheese has genuine pricing power. No pricing power = no moat.

control_groups.conclusion: PASS — earnings yield ~4.5%, borderline. But moat (Colgate toothpaste #1 globally) is far stronger than Kraft.
BUY — passes all gates. Chocolate brand with genuine pricing power, consumers trade up, holiday/emotional consumption stickiness.

buffett_action: Berkshire + 3G Capital engineered the Kraft-Heinz merger, Berkshire invested ~$5B additional cash
actual_result: $15.4B goodwill impairment 2018, SEC investigation, dividend cut, stock fell from $90→$25. Berkshire lost ~$20B+ on paper.
key_insight: Gates ④⑤⑥⑦ all triggered simultaneously — rare case of multiple failures. Volume declining while price rises is the classic sign of a brand losing relevance. Hershey passes the same framework: the difference is genuine vs. legacy brand.
